proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65; presumably age) is positively correlated with pfs_months — patients with higher feature_078 have longer progression-free survival.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.766 between feature_078 and pfs_months (p ~ 0). Mean feature_078 by pfs quintile rises monotonically from 53.4 (lowest pfs quintile) to 75.5 (highest). feature_078 is the dominant single predictor of pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 80 binary features, feature_051 is associated with a clinically meaningful reduction in pfs_months relative to patients without feature_051.
feature_038 is associated with longer pfs_months relative to patients without feature_038.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 (n=27,481, 55%) mean pfs = 3.67 vs feature_051=0 (n=22,519) mean pfs = 5.02; difference = -1.352 months, Welch t-test p ~ 0. Largest binary main effect in the cohort.
feature_038=1 (n=10,022, 20%) mean pfs = 5.09 vs feature_038=0 (n=39,978) mean pfs = 4.12; difference = +0.972 months, Welch t-test p = 1.7e-216.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature_057 (levels 0, 1, 2) is monotonically and negatively associated with pfs_months — higher feature_057 levels correspond to shorter pfs.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0 (n=17,592) mean pfs = 5.27, level 1 (n=24,971) mean = 4.05, level 2 (n=7,437) mean = 2.91. Spearman rho = -0.360, p ~ 0. Strong monotonic negative association — likely a disease-stage or risk-tier variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the 5 race categories in feature_064 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs by race: white 4.32 (n=32,456), black 4.27 (n=5,996), hispanic 4.31 (n=7,572), asian 4.31 (n=3,003), other 4.41 (n=973). One-way ANOVA F=0.87, p=0.479. No detectable race main effect on pfs.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the 4 insurance categories in feature_018 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs by insurance: medicare 4.31, private 4.33, medicaid 4.28, uninsured 4.25. F=1.48, p=0.219. No detectable insurance main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on the strongest univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_067, feature_057, feature_099, feature_092) and demographics will retain large signed effects for the top binaries while explaining most of the variance.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_057 + feature_099 + feature_092 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018) + feature_109 + feature_067', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.857. Adjusted coefficients (signed effect on pfs_months): feature_078 +0.176/unit (t=451), feature_051 -1.361 (t=-175), feature_038 +0.943 (t=97), feature_013 -0.354 (t=-44), feature_043 -0.287 (t=-35), feature_057 -1.171/level (t=-205), feature_099 -0.076/unit, feature_092 +0.470/unit, feature_109 -0.357, feature_067 +0.021 (NS, p=0.357). All race and insurance coefficients NS (each |t|<1.3).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is amplified at higher levels of feature_078 (i.e., feature_078 × feature_051 has a negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_078:feature_051 = -0.0070 per unit (t=-7.94, p=2.0e-15). Main effect of feature_051 at feature_078=0 is -0.901; at feature_078=65 the implied effect is -1.36; at feature_078=90 it is -1.53. Older patients experience a larger absolute pfs decrement from feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is modified by feature_078 (interaction differs from zero).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_078:feature_038 = +0.0012 per unit (t=1.08, p=0.281). No detectable modification of feature_038 benefit by feature_078 — the +0.94 month gain appears uniform across the age range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 and feature_038 interact multiplicatively — patients with both have a different combined effect than the sum of individual effects.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_051:feature_038 = +0.029 (t=1.34, p=0.179). The two effects are statistically additive — having both feature_051 and feature_038 yields close to the sum (~ -1.36 + 0.94 = -0.42 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 on pfs_months is amplified with increasing feature_078 (significant negative feature_078 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_057 = -0.0074 per unit (t=-11.6, p=4.4e-31). At feature_078=50 the per-level effect of feature_057 is approximately -1.06; at feature_078=80 it is approximately -1.28.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 and feature_057 interact: the marginal effect of feature_051 differs by feature_057 level.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_057 = +0.045 per level (t=3.47, p=5.2e-04). Statistically significant but modest attenuation — the negative impact of feature_051 is slightly smaller at higher feature_057 levels (likely a ceiling effect because pfs is already short).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 benefit is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (negative feature_038 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_038:feature_057 = -0.058 per level (t=-3.66, p=2.5e-04). Mean pfs gain from feature_038 within strata: level 0 +0.93, level 1 +1.00, level 2 +0.90 months — overall benefit is preserved, with mild interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 and feature_043 are not additive — they show a strong positive multiplicative interaction so that having both is no worse than having only one.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013*feature_043 + feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_057', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Main feature_013 = -0.507, feature_043 = -0.494, interaction = +0.510 (t=28.6, p=1.8e-178). Implied conditional means relative to neither: 13-only ≈ -0.51, 43-only ≈ -0.49, both ≈ -0.49. Highly subadditive (essentially redundant) effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, mean 3.84, range 0-24.6) interacts negatively with feature_078: the deleterious effect of feature_099 on pfs is larger in older patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*feature_099 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_057', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_099 = -0.00044 per unit-pair (p=3.9e-05). Confirms a small but real age-dependent amplification of feature_099 toxicity on pfs.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) interacts positively with feature_078: the protective effect of feature_092 grows with age.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*feature_092 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_057', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_092 = +0.0035 per unit-pair (p=3.1e-05). At feature_078=50 the per-unit feature_092 effect is +0.42; at feature_078=80 it is +0.52.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs decrement attributable to feature_051 differs by self-reported race (feature_064) — i.e., there is treatment-effect or exposure-effect heterogeneity by race.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_064)*feature_051 + feature_078', df).fit(); race-stratified Welch tests
analyses.result_summary: Stratified mean differences (feature_051=1 vs 0) by race: white -1.341, black -1.347, hispanic -1.389, asian -1.334, other -1.521. Joint Wald F-test for feature_064×feature_051 interaction terms: F=1.29, p=0.271. No significant race-based heterogeneity in the feature_051 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs decrement attributable to feature_051 differs by insurance status (feature_018).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified mean differences by insurance: medicare -1.331, private -1.357, medicaid -1.404, uninsured -1.340. Joint feature_018×feature_051 Wald F=2.00, p=0.112. No significant insurance-based heterogeneity in feature_051 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 reduces pfs_months in every age stratum (<55, 55–70, ≥70), and the absolute reduction is larger in the older strata.

analyses.code: for ag in [(0,55),(55,70),(70,100)]: stratified Welch t-test on feature_051
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 mean diff (1 vs 0): age <55 (n=7,995) = -1.214 (p=6.7e-299); 55–70 (n=26,505) = -1.364 (p~0); ≥70 (n=15,500) = -1.390 (p~0). Effect monotonically larger with age, consistent with the previously identified feature_078×feature_051 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 confers a positive pfs benefit within every feature_057 stratum (0, 1, 2).

analyses.code: stratified Welch t-test of feature_038 within feature_057 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 mean diff within feature_057 strata: level 0 (n=17,592) +0.925 (p=5.2e-76); level 1 (n=24,971) +0.995 (p=3.8e-122); level 2 (n=7,437) +0.896 (p=2.9e-34). Benefit is consistent across disease-severity strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The prevalence of the major treatment-like binary features (feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_067) differs by race (feature_064) — indicating utilization disparity.

analyses.result_summary: Chi-square p-values for race-by-feature: feature_051 p=0.382, feature_038 p=0.550, feature_013 p=0.304, feature_043 p=0.632, feature_109 p=0.652, feature_067 p=0.756. No detectable race-based utilization disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The prevalence of the major treatment-like binary features differs by insurance (feature_018).

analyses.result_summary: Chi-square p-values for insurance-by-feature: feature_051 p=0.505, feature_038 p=0.591, feature_013 p=0.900, feature_043 p=0.692, feature_109 p=0.758, feature_067 p=0.128. No detectable insurance-based utilization disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_092 and feature_099, race (feature_064) jointly predicts pfs_months.

analyses.code: joint Wald F-test on race coefficients in the full model
analyses.result_summary: Joint race F=0.55, p=0.700. Individual coefficients: black -0.001 (p=0.95), hispanic +0.0002 (p=0.99), other +0.022 (p=0.50), white -0.009 (p=0.57). Race contributes no incremental predictive value after adjustment for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the same clinical covariates, insurance (feature_018) jointly predicts pfs_months.

analyses.code: joint Wald F-test on insurance coefficients in the full model
analyses.result_summary: Joint insurance F=0.55, p=0.647. Individual coefficients: medicare -0.008 (p=0.52), private -0.004 (p=0.74), uninsured -0.027 (p=0.22). Insurance does not predict pfs after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months has a positive quadratic component (i.e., the marginal pfs benefit per unit increase in feature_078 grows with feature_078).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + I(feature_078**2) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_057', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Linear term +0.091 (t=22.0), quadratic term +0.00065 (t=20.5, p<1e-90). Convex (accelerating) age-pfs relationship: implied per-unit slope rises from ~0.16 at feature_078=50 to ~0.21 at feature_078=85.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all 80 binary features are entered together with the strongest continuous and ordinal predictors, only a small set (feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109) remain significant at p<0.001 — most binaries are noise.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_057 + ' + ' + '.join(all_80_binaries), df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full 84-predictor model R^2 = 0.857 (essentially identical to the parsimonious model). Binaries surviving p<0.001: feature_051 -1.361, feature_013 -0.355, feature_038 +0.943, feature_043 -0.287, feature_109 -0.356. The remaining 75 binaries show no independent signal — confirming that the bulk of the explainable variance is captured by feature_078, feature_057, and a five-binary parsimonious panel.
